summary: Iteration 169: Tested Terminal Signature Dialog fix (apiInstance prop) + Inline Credit Type Selection (4-button grid). All 7 backend tests pass (100%). All frontend UI tests pass. Both bug fixes verified end-to-end.

integration_issues.issue: CropCreditTypeDialog uses AuthContext api (import { api } from '../contexts/AuthContext') for internal API calls (checking existing crop credits). In terminal context, AuthContext has no valid web-app token → 403 on /api/crop-credits. This is a PRE-EXISTING issue, not introduced by the current fix. The dialog UI still renders correctly.

action_items: MINOR: resetCheckout() in TerminalSales.jsx (line 447-451) does not reset cropCreditConfig or cropTypeDialog state. If a Charged-to-Crop sale FAILS (400 from API), cropCreditConfig remains set on next checkout open. Fix: add setCropCreditConfig(null) and setCropTypeDialog(false) inside resetCheckout().

MINOR: CropCreditTypeDialog uses AuthContext api for its own crop-credit check. In terminal context this will 403 if no web-app login. To fully fix, pass apiInstance prop to CropCreditTypeDialog as well (same pattern as RequestSignatureDialog). Pre-existing issue.
critical_code_review_comments: RequestSignatureDialog.js: All 5 API calls (lines 102, 125, 187, 206, 215) now use http. instead of api. — FIX CONFIRMED CORRECT.
TerminalSales.jsx line 1516: apiInstance={api} passed to RequestSignatureDialog — FIX CONFIRMED CORRECT.
TerminalSales.jsx lines 1087-1108: 4-button 2x2 grid (15 days, 30 days, 60 days, Charged to Crop) with correct data-testids — FIX CONFIRMED CORRECT.
TerminalSales.jsx lines 1211-1224: Confirm button calls processSale() directly, no setCropTypeDialog(true) — FIX CONFIRMED CORRECT.
MINOR: resetCheckout() missing setCropCreditConfig(null) — can cause stale crop config on failed sale recovery.

MINOR: CropCreditTypeDialog still uses AuthContext api (not terminal api) for its internal /crop-credits check — causes 403 in terminal context. Same antipattern that was fixed for RequestSignatureDialog.
seed_data_creation: 2 test signature sessions created in MongoDB (TEST_SignatureCustomer, TEST_StatusCustomer) — will expire in 5 minutes
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both fixes verified: (1) RequestSignatureDialog.js uses http=apiInstance||api for ALL calls — no raw api. remains. (2) TerminalSales.jsx has 4-button credit grid (data-testid credit-days-15/30/60, credit-type-crop). Charged-to-Crop opens CropCreditTypeDialog immediately. Confirm button calls processSale() directly without triggering crop dialog. Backend: POST /api/signatures/session requires auth (401/403 without) and credit_context (400 if missing). Minor: resetCheckout() should also reset cropCreditConfig and cropTypeDialog. CropCreditTypeDialog still uses AuthContext api (403 in terminal).

rca_of_issues.signature_not_authenticated: RequestSignatureDialog was importing and calling api from AuthContext for all HTTP calls. In terminal context, AuthContext has no valid token (terminal uses its own axios instance). Fix: added apiInstance prop (line 34), created http=apiInstance||api fallback (line 54), replaced all 5 raw api.* calls with http.* calls. TerminalSales passes apiInstance={api} (terminal's axios) to the dialog.
rca_of_issues.crop_credit_inline: Previously CropCreditTypeDialog was opened by the confirm button (setCropTypeDialog(true) inside onClick). Now the 4th button 'Charged to Crop' (credit-type-crop) directly calls setCropTypeDialog(true). Confirm button now calls processSale() directly. Consistent with 15/30/60 day buttons which set state and proceed.
